section_anchor: Dampening interventions: what each mod targets
brief_summary: Four-panel horizontal strip, one panel per dampening method (PE foam, tape mod, o-ring mod, silicone pour). Each panel shows a small cross-section sketch of the mod's physical position in the stack, a name, a one-line description of what layer it targets, and a small acoustic waveform sketch indicating which frequency band is most affected. All panels bordered in ochre.
data_sources: apps/web/src/content/articles/keyboard-acoustics-deep-dive.mdx:Dampening interventions — 'The PE foam mod places a thin polyethylene sheet between the PCB and the case bottom. It targets the air cavity below the PCB that acts as a resonant chamber.'
apps/web/src/content/articles/keyboard-acoustics-deep-dive.mdx:Dampening interventions — 'The tape mod applies painter’s tape to the back face of the PCB... a slight reduction in PCB-surface ring.'

apps/web/src/content/articles/keyboard-acoustics-deep-dive.mdx:Dampening interventions — 'The o-ring mod inserts silicone O-rings between the PCB mounting screws and the standoffs. It targets the rigid standoff-to-PCB contact points.'

apps/web/src/content/articles/keyboard-acoustics-deep-dive.mdx:Dampening interventions — 'Silicone pours address case cavity resonance at the most fundamental level by eliminating the resonant volume entirely.'
